feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: Marcus Smart declines player option and will enter unrestricted free agency
entry.content: Marcus Smart declines player option and will enter unrestricted free agency

entry.title: Ben Simmons planning NBA comeback, mentions Miami and Philadelphia as potential landing spots
entry.content: Ben Simmons planning NBA comeback, mentions Miami and Philadelphia as potential landing spots

entry.title: “You can’t be in a state where you’re in a high-stress …
entry.content: “You can’t be in a state where you’re in a high-stress …

entry.title: Resident doctors in England accept government offer on pay and jobs
entry.content: British Medical Association called off a strike at the last minute last month to put the offer to membersResident doctors in England have voted to accept a new government deal on pay and jobs.It comes after the British Medical Association called off a strike at the last minute last month to put the offer to members. Continue reading...

entry.title: Supreme Court won't hear Trump's appeal of E. Jean Carroll sexual abuse case
entry.content: A unanimous federal jury found that a preponderance of evidence supported Carroll's claim that Mr. Trump sexually abused her.

entry.title: Tech giants are not going to slash their AI spending plans, bullish tech analyst says
entry.content: Tech giants are not going to slash their AI spending plans, bullish tech analyst says

entry.title: Supreme Court Blocks Trump From Firing Fed Governor Lisa Cook
entry.content: In a pair of decisions, presidential authority over independent agencies expands—but the central bank is the notable exception.

entry.title: See How the Supreme Court Has Decided the Major Cases of This Term
entry.content: A visual roundup of a Supreme Court term marked by politically charged disputes, including tests of presidential power, LGBTQ rights and U.S. election laws.

entry.title: Supreme Court expands presidential firing power, overturning 90-year-old ruling
entry.content: The Supreme Court overturned a 90-year-old decision that allowed Congress to shield members of certain independent agencies from being fired by the president at will.

entry.title: Is Real Brokerage (REAX) the Best High Growth Penny Stock to Buy?
entry.content: Is Real Brokerage (REAX) the Best High Growth Penny Stock to Buy?

entry.title: Comstock (LODE) Selects Ohio for Solar Panel Recycling Facility
entry.content: Comstock (LODE) Selects Ohio for Solar Panel Recycling Facility

entry.title: Afghanistan's Taliban claims Pakistani airstrikes killed 36 civilians
entry.content: Pakistani security forces carried out an intelligence-based ground operation along the Pakistan-Afghanistan​ border, followed by "calibrated strikes."

entry.title: Venezuela earthquakes aftershock hits near capital city as man rescued from rubble after being trapped for 106 hours – latest updates
entry.content: No damage reported in 4.6 magnitude aftershock; At least 1,450 people are known to have died in initial quakes but number is expected to riseIn an update to X, El Salvador’s president has said that after hours of intensive work rescuers have freed Aaron Levi Cantillo Vargas, 21, who was trapped under a building in Caraballeda, La Guaira, calling the operation “a miracle”.“This rescue was made possible thanks to the coordinated effort of the rescue teams from Venezuela, Mexico, and El Salvador, who worked tirelessly to reach Aaron,” Nayib Bukele wrote in a social media post, adding that the 21-year-old is now receiving specialised medical attention. Continue reading...

entry.title: Ex-NBA players Malik Beasley, Ed Davis indicted in gambling probe
entry.content: Former NBA players Malik Beasley and Ed Davis have been indicted on illegal sports gambling charges, authorities announced Monday.

entry.title: Malik Beasley indicted in latest round of charges in DOJ sports gambling probe
entry.content: Malik Beasley, who played in the NBA for nine years, was indicted Monday alongside former player Ed Davis in the latest round of charges by the Justice Department as part of its gambling investigation, officials said. CBS News' Anna Schecter has more.

entry.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump bid to fire Fed's Lisa Cook as legal case continues
entry.content: The Supreme Court allowed Lisa Cook to continue in her post as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors while legal proceedings over President Trump's attempt to fire her continue.

entry.title: Luigi Mangione due in federal court for pretrial hearing
entry.content: Luigi Mangione is set to be in federal court on Monday for a pretrial hearing. CBS News' Anna Schecter has the latest.

entry.title: Half of social media child safety features don't work, report claims
entry.content: A new report found half of social media safety features intended to keep children safe online do not work as claimed.

entry.title: Supreme Court Won’t Hear Trump Appeal of E. Jean Carroll Sex-Abuse Verdict
entry.content: The high court’s rejection leaves intact $5 million in damages awarded by a New York federal jury.

entry.title: WhatsApp introduces usernames so you can chat without phone numbers
entry.content: WhatsApp is launching usernames later this year, but you can reserve yours starting this week.

entry.title: Supreme Court Lets States Count Mailed Ballots Received After Election Day
entry.content: The decision rebuffs the Republican Party’s efforts to end grace periods for some late-arriving votes.

entry.title: Don't like Gemini? Here's how to roll back to Google Assistant on your Android phone
entry.content: Want to go back to your ex... AI assistant? We don't blame you. Take these steps to get Google Assistant back after your fling with Gemini.

entry.title: Comcast spins off NBCUniversal as it exits media entirely
entry.content: Comcast plans to spin out NBCUniversal and leave the entertainment business.

entry.title: Eagles announce date for public practice, single-game ticket details
entry.content: Eagles single-game tickets for the 2026 season and public practice tickets at Lincoln Financial Field will go on sale Tuesday at 10 a.m.

entry.title: Is Patriots WR A.J. Brown dark horse to win 2026 offensive MVP award?
entry.content: Patriots wide receiver A.J. Brown could be in a position to put up some big numbers in the 2026 season.

entry.title: Wimbledon 2026 men’s and women’s seeds list and tracker
entry.content: Jannik Sinner and Aryna Sabalenka helm the men’s and women’s draws as Wimbledon gets underway

entry.title: Padres re-sign former Yankees 2nd-round draft pick infielder
entry.content: The second baseman has hit 21 career home runs.

entry.title: Real Madrid star silencing critics with World Cup defensive numbers
entry.content: One of the main problems identified by observers in the Real Madrid team’s style of play during the last season was the presence of two of the biggest stars in European football, i.e., Kylian Mbappe...

entry.title: 'He’s been a legend that inspires': McCullum pays tribute to Ben Stokes' legacy after retirement announcement
entry.content: In a heartfelt announcement, England's Test captain Ben Stokes has decided to retire from Test cricket, bringing down the curtain on a stellar career. Head coach Brendon McCullum commended Stokes for his significant influence on the game, emphasizing his knack for turning matches around and motivating emerging talents in cricket. Stokes leaves behind a legacy featuring more than 7,000 runs and 252 wickets, ensuring his place in cricket history.

entry.title: When is Conor McGregor vs Max Holloway? Date, start time, card and how to watch UFC 329 fight
entry.content: McGregor’s first fight in five years, after he suffered a broken leg in 2021, sees him face old rival Max Holloway

entry.title: Nick Bongard wins Piggly Wiggly high school athlete of the week
entry.content: With over 53% of last week's vote, Nicolet's Nick Bongard won the last Piggly Wiggly high school athlete of the week vote for the 2025-26 school year.

entry.title: Should you still worry about OLED burn-In in 2026?
entry.content: Burn-in is often overblown, but understanding the phenomenon is still important.

entry.title: Families of two football players killed and injured in Venezuela earthquakes
entry.content: Lucas Trejo of Argentina loses wife and two children while Héctor Bello’s partner dies saving their daughter‘Critical hours’ in Venezuela earthquake rescue operation – latest updatesFamily members of two professional footballers are among those killed by the twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela in a catastrophe that has claimed at least 1,450 lives.Among the reported victims are the wife and two children of Lucas Trejo, an Argentinian footballer signed to Club Sport Marítimo of La Guaira. He searched through rubble in the coastal city for his wife, Yanina, and their children, Aaron and Ainhoa, for three days before their bodies were recovered by rescue workers. Continue reading...

entry.title: Comcast is spinning off NBCUniversal media and entertainment assets
entry.content: Comcast is spinning off its NBCUniversal entertainment and media assets into a separate publicly traded company.

entry.title: U.S.-Iran talks to resume after exchange of strikes, Trump says
entry.content: President Trump says U.S.-Iran talks will resume, at Tehran's request, after several days of tit-for-tat strikes tested a shaky ceasefire.

entry.title: Comcast plans to split into 2 companies, spinning off NBCUniversal
entry.content: Current shareholders would receive shares in both companies under the planned split, Comcast said Monday.

entry.title: Thousands of Venezuelans still missing after earthquakes as search for survivors continues
entry.content: Tens of thousands of people remain missing after two powerful earthquakes rocked Venezuela last week. There is now a global effort to find survivors, including crews from the U.S. Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.

entry.title: Huge Supreme Court decisions imminent as term comes to an end
entry.content: Several high-profile Supreme Court rulings are expected as the court nears the end of its term, including a decision on President Trump's bid to end birthright citizenship. CBS News' Jan Crawford reports.

entry.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll keeps climbing, voices pierce through the rubble
entry.content: The death toll keeps climbing in Venezuela after back-to-back earthquakes rocked the South American region. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez has more as rescue teams dig for survivors.

entry.title: Soccer star's wife and 2 children killed in Venezuela earthquakes, team says
entry.content: The wife and two children of Argentine soccer star Lucas Trejo died after powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela, his team said.

entry.title: Venezuelans race against clock to rescue earthquake survivors
entry.content: Venezuelans are desperately searching for survivors after the country faced catastrophic, back-to-back deadly earthquakes. Claudia Manresa, a Project HOPE regional manager, joins with more details on rescue efforts.

entry.title: Weather tracker: North-west US hit by snow ahead of eastern heatwave
entry.content: Western states experience unseasonally low temperatures but New York and Washington DC could reach 40C by end of weekUnseasonal snow has fallen in some parts of the US, while other parts of the country brace for a heatwave this week.A strong cold front spread into the western US from the northern Pacific over the weekend, bringing an abrupt change in conditions to a region that had been experiencing high summer temperatures amid drought. Temperatures from the Canadian border to California have widely been 5-10C below the norm since Friday, and more than 10C below in some parts farther north. The pattern is expected to remain for much of the coming week. Continue reading...

entry.title: Bill Cassidy blasts Trump and RFK Jr. as he prepares to leave Senate
entry.content: Louisiana Republican Sen. Bill Cassidy is denouncing President Trump's administration, including Secretary of Health and Human Services Robert F. Kennedy Jr., despite being the key vote to advance Kennedy's nomination last year. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi has more.

entry.title: Rocket Lab buys satellite company Iridium to go up against Starlink and Amazon's Leo
entry.content: Rocket Lab will own satellites connecting the most remote corners of the Earth.

entry.title: Oura Ring 5 review: Size is everything
entry.content: Oura took its class-leading smart ring, made it 40 percent smaller and made the battery last longer.

entry.title: Google reportedly capped Meta's use of Gemini AI for coding and chatbots
entry.content: Google was forced to cap Meta's use of Gemini AI due to a lack of capacity.

entry.title: Analysis-What's good for the US economy now may not be good for stocks
entry.content: Analysis-What's good for the US economy now may not be good for stocks

entry.title: Five Americans die every hour from toxic vehicle emissions, study finds
entry.content: Research suggests 41,800 premature US deaths in 2024 were attributable to road pollutionRoughly five Americans die every hour due to exposure to toxic road vehicle pollution, a new study has found.It’s the latest warning showing fossil-fueled transit is a major driver of mortality. Continue reading...

entry.title: Americans' pride in US history and democracy drops, and fewer are proud to be American, polls find
entry.content: Americans' pride in US history and democracy drops, and fewer are proud to be American, polls find

entry.title: DJI's Osmo Pocket 4P promises 17 stops of dynamic range
entry.content: DJI's Osmo Pocket 4P introduces a new D-Log2 format and promises 17 stops of dynamic range.

entry.title: Nvidia's AI chip sales in China stall, as local chipmakers like Huawei take the lead
entry.content: In the race between the U.S. and China to develop artificial intelligence, the battle over hardware and computing power is heating up as Chinese companies like Huawei overtake global industry leaders like Nvidia in their home market

entry.title: Mikel Brown Jr. is excited to bring his rivalry with Darius Acuff Jr. to the NBA
entry.content: Mikel Brown Jr. and Darius Acuff Jr. have been linked since high school, so it seems that dynamic will continue in the NBA.

entry.title: What time is Brazil vs. Japan? Live stream, TV channel, kickoff schedule for World Cup 2026 match
entry.content: Here's everything you need to know to watch the Brazil vs. Japan World Cup match, including where to watch on TV or live stream, what time the game starts and more.

entry.title: John Wall has fun with AJ Dybantsa's arrival with the Washington Wizards
entry.content: AJ Dybantsa is getting a huge welcome to Washington, D.C., including some fun moments with a Wizards legend.

entry.title: Former NBA champion believes LaMelo Ball and Anthony Edwards are the NBA's best backcourt
entry.content: LaMelo Ball and Anthony Edwards could be the duo that the NBA goes crazy for.

entry.title: Why Jaydon Blue could become Cowboys starting running back in 2026
entry.content: Could the Texas rookie bounce back into a starting job?

entry.title: Kendrick Perkins calls out the Lakers for building around Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves
entry.content: The Lakers still have some star power with Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves, but Kendrick Perkins is not a big fan.

entry.title: Iran threatens "complete halt" to talks; U.S. official says they will resume
entry.content: Iran launched drone and missile attacks targeting Kuwait and Bahrain on Sunday after the U.S. and Iran traded attacks earlier over the weekend.

entry.title: 6/28/2026: Betting on War; The Looting of Cambodia
entry.content: First, high win rate of bets on military operations a likely sign of insider trading. Then, a report on Cambodia tracking down looted antiquities.

entry.title: LeBron James and Anthony Davis rumored to reunite on surprise team
entry.content: LeBron's next stop could be his best team ever.

entry.title: Mariners trade for versatile infielder who will be joining his 7th MLB team after Giants, Dodgers, Blue Jays, Phillies, Tigers, Diamondbacks
entry.content: Seattle is adding some depth on the dirt.

entry.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
entry.content: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

entry.title: Canada advances as the World Cup knockout round gets underway
entry.content: Canada beat South Africa 1-0 Sunday as the World Cup's knockout stage kicked off. Shanelle Kaul has more.

entry.title: A 35-foot fried apple pie at a McDonald's on Route 66
entry.content: Noel Brennan takes a look at some of the history of fast food in the United States and visits a 35-foot fried apple pie at a McDonald's on Route 66.

entry.title: Union Pacific Big Boy, the largest, most powerful steam locomotive in the world, tours the U.S.
entry.content: As part of America's 250th birthday celebration, Union Pacific is sending Big Boy, the largest, heaviest and most powerful steam locomotive in the world, across the country. Ian Lee reports.

entry.title: Iran launches strikes targeting Bahrain, Kuwait, threatens "complete halt" to talks
entry.content: Iran on Sunday launched missiles and drones towards U.S. military sites in the region. A U.S. official says no casualties were reported. Iran says the attacks were in retaliation for U.S. airstrikes that hit the Islamic Republic, and threatened a "complete halt" could come to negotiations to end the war if Washington continues its attacks. Aaron Navarro reports.

entry.title: Videos show U.S. rescue team pull mother, baby from rubble in Venezuela
entry.content: Hundreds of U.S. search and rescue workers are on the ground in Venezuela after deadly quakes struck the South American nation.

entry.title: The Baby Formula Probe Produced a Pile of Evidence. Then the DOJ Dropped the Case.
entry.content: Some prosecutors believed they had enough to charge Abbott Laboratories, but senior officials opted for civil settlement.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
All things are difficult before they are easy.
Lucky Numbers: 54, 35, 6, 34, 73, 52

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Use a rubber band to prevent a door from latching.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: Venus is the only planet that rotates clockwise.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Jim Rohn
entry.content: “All leaders are readers.” — Jim Rohn

entry.title: Photos show Pride around the world
entry.content:
Born out of the 1969 Stonewall uprising in New York, Pride Month has grown into a global celebration of LGBTQ+ communities, with marches and festivals taking place in cities across the Americas, Europe, Africa and Asia. The month reached its peak Sunday with landmark Pride parades in New York and San Francisco.
___
This is a photo gallery curated by AP photo editors.

entry.title: Trump says his renovation plans for a golf course will have Washington hosting a 'major' tournament
entry.content: President Donald Trump surveyed his construction projects around the nation's capital, suggesting his redevelopment of the East Potomac Golf Links could host major tournaments

entry.title: U.S. and Iran Agree to Stop Fight Over the Strait of Hormuz
entry.content: The two sides are set to resume peace talks intended to strike a long-term deal to end hostilities and restrain Tehran’s nuclear program.

entry.title: Russians Feel the War’s Hardships as Ukraine Pummels Crimea
entry.content: Strikes on fuel and power infrastructure have cut electricity in parts of Crimea, strained water supplies and pushed residents to the black market for gasoline.

entry.title: Venezuela Is Desperately Searching for 50,000 Missing After Earthquakes
entry.content: Four days later, hope is fading for families and teams looking for signs of life as criticism of Delcy Rodríguez’s government grows.

entry.title: The Company Cleaning Up the Reflecting Pool Says It Has ‘Nothing to Hide’
entry.content: Greenwater Services got a no-bid contract and is partly owned by a twice-convicted felon who is a Trump friend.

entry.title: Trump Adds a Washington Golf Course Renovation to His Fall Agenda
entry.content: The president said he would keep East Potomac Golf Links open to the public after rebuilding its course.

entry.title: Kate completes Three Peaks challenge to raise money for cancer charity
entry.content: Princess of Wales becomes first royal to climb Scotland, England and Wales’s highest mountains, 18 months after cancer remissionThe Princess of Wales has completed the Three Peaks challenge to raise money for a cancer charity, becoming the first member of the royal family to achieve the feat.Catherine Middleton, 44, revealed in a message on social media she had successfully taken on the trek not only as a physical endeavour but “to give something back” and raise awareness of the wider impact of serious illness. Continue reading...

entry.title: 'Horrific': At least 117 dead dogs found at California 'no-kill' shelter
entry.content: Many of the dead dogs had gunshot wounds, authorities said.

entry.title: Khadijah Farrakhan, 'first lady of Nation of Islam,' dies at 90
entry.content: “Mother Khadijah” worked alongside her charismatic husband for decades.

entry.title: How a Romford maternity ward was able to turn the tide after a ‘requires improvement’ rating
entry.content: Hiring more staff and providing interpreters are just some of the actions taken by Queen’s hospital to tackle core problems• Risk of serious birth injuries is rising for women in England, data suggestsTwo days after giving birth, Juliana Nascimento Barbosa is still ecstatic about becoming a mother. “I’m so happy to have my baby,” she says from her bed in Queen’s hospital in Romford, Essex, smiling broadly.To her left, her husband, Emerson, sits on a chair. To her right, their newborn son Dominic lies on a neonatal resuscitaire receiving phototherapy, a light treatment to help relieve his jaundice. Continue reading...

entry.title: Risk of serious birth injuries is rising for women in England, data suggests
entry.content: About 31 in every 1,000 facing haemorrhage or severe tears, NHS figures show, in trend described as ‘national crisis’How a Romford maternity ward was able to turn the tide after a ‘requires improvement’ ratingWomen in England are at their highest risk of suffering a serious injury while giving birth since records began in 2020, NHS figures show.The rate of women sustaining the most serious type of tear during childbirth rose to 31.1 in every 1,000 in January, February and March – the highest since monitoring began in 2020. Continue reading...

entry.title: 5 easy ways to get more range out of your EV
entry.content: These little tricks will help you spend more time driving instead of charging.

entry.title: Military searches for a Marine missing off California coast
entry.content: A Marine went missing during a training exercise off southern California.

entry.title: Kaine says guardrails on Pentagon firings could see bipartisan support
entry.content: Sen. Tim Kaine said guardrails on Pentagon firings could see bipartisan support in Congress, following a string of high-level officers exiting the military during the second Trump administration.

entry.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy accuses RFK Jr. of building public health "upon a foundation of lies"
entry.content: Sen. Bill Cassidy, who delivered the key vote to advance Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s nomination, told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that "if you build public health upon a foundation of lies, then you're going to have the absence of adequate public health."

entry.title: Transcript: Sen. Tim Kaine on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," June 28, 2026
entry.content: The following is the transcript of an interview with Sen. Tim Kaine, Democrat of Virginia, that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on June 28, 2026.

entry.title: Jan Crawford says remaining Supreme Court decisions could be a "mixed bag" for Trump
entry.content: Jan Crawford, CBS News' chief legal correspondent, says that she believes the remaining Supreme Court decisions will be a "mixed bag" for President Trump.

entry.title: Face the Nation: Kaine, Cassidy, Crawford
entry.content: Missed the second half of the show? Sens. Tim Kaine and Bill Cassidy and CBS News chief legal correspondent Jan Crawford join.

entry.title: Australia doubles the maximum penalty for its social media ban
entry.content: The fine can now potentially hit 99 million AUD, or $68 million.

entry.title: Fall in NHS waiting lists is not a Labour win | Letter
entry.content: Priscilla Alderson asks how long the NHS will survive under a Burnham-Streeting governmentA fall in NHS waiting lists is presented as a government success (From the NHS to new homes, Starmer’s successes and setbacks, 23 June). NHS authorities have paid providers £33 per patient to remove thousands of them from the lists, many still in great need, and a survey of nearly 2,600 people in England found 16% had used the private sector in the past year.Fewer names on the lists help the government to seem to “reduce backlogs” and “meet waiting time targets”. Continue reading...

entry.title: Heat wave and high humidity will blast much of the U.S. this week, weather service says
entry.content: A long and dangerous heat wave will blast a large swath of the United States this week, the National Weather Service said, with temperatures rising ahead of the July Fourth holiday and feeling even hotter because of the high humidity that's arriving with it.

entry.title: Sen. Tim Kaine says guardrails on Pentagon firings could see bipartisan support in Congress
entry.content: Democratic Sen. Tim Kaine of Virginia told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that he believes there could be bipartisan support for guardrails on Pentagon firings in the aftermath of the departure of Gen. Chris Donahue, the commander of U.S. Army Europe and Africa.

entry.title: 5 nurses among 11 killed in French skydiving plane crash
entry.content: Mayor Mathieu Klein said the victims "died in full view of their loved ones, who were preparing to film the tandem skydives."

entry.title: Douglas Brinkley on America at 250: History tells us hoping for unity is not futile
entry.content: In these fiercely-polarized times, the presidential historian reminds us that Americans' freedom has been tested – and has survived – much worse.

entry.title: Apple's touchscreen MacBook reportedly won't wait for the M7 chips
entry.content: The new models will launch with the M5 Pro and M5 Max, according to Mark Gurman.

entry.title: TechCrunch Mobility: All eyes on Tesla FSD
entry.content: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

entry.title: ‘Treating children like cattle’: what happens when private equity takes over a UK care home?
entry.content: Experts raise alarm as firms aiming to maximise assets and sell for profit become more involved in vital public services‘Financial pandemic’: £1 in every £11 spent on UK public contractors goes to private equityNurseries, vets and shops: the sectors where private equity plays a big rolePrivate equity companies have slowly and invisibly increased their involvement in public services – from elderly care homes to fostering placements for vulnerable children – with some unintended consequences.Take Compass Community, a private equity-backed firm which provides children’s homes, fostering services and schooling for children with special educational needs (Send). Continue reading...
